issue_title: Account console does not work with `--http-relative-path`
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

The router defined in account console assumes fixed pathname /realms/_realm_/account, however when using `--http-relative-path`, this changes significantly. For example, the testsuite uses `/auth` prefix, and the account console only displays error when navigating to `/auth/realms/master/account/`.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Account console is displayed

### Actual behavior

Account console is not loaded

### How to Reproduce?

Run keycloak with `--http-relative-path=/auth` and navigate to http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/master/account/


### Anything else?

Caused by #22192, the fix #27604 was assuming only default locations.

Use correct path to account console

Fixes: #27709

issue_title: User profile fields cannot be set empty once they have a non-empty value (in Login Theme)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

I have observed that it's currently not possible to set a non-empty field to empty by means of the "Update profile" action (and I guess the same applies to the other Login Theme actions).
To be more precise, the UI allows to set an empty value (when the field is not required), but the empty value is not stored at server side. In both Admin and Account UI the old (non-empty) value is still displayed.

### Version

23.0.7 AND current main branch (commit 1c0c56e8055ef227d3aa72102a2acb10eb306d16)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

- When a field is not required, it should be possible to set the field to empty, and the empty value SHOULD BE persisted.

### Actual behavior

When a field is not required, it is possible to set the field to empty, but the empty value is NOT persisted.

### How to Reproduce?

- In the Admin UI (or via Admin API): 
  - Add a simple optional attribute to a realm's user profile - let's call it `test`.
  - Create a user at the realm, set the required action "Update Profile" for the user, and initialize the `test` attribute value with "anything".
- In the Login Theme / Account UI:
  - Login at the Account UI with the user.
  - The "Update Profile" screen should be displayed with `test` attribute value "anything".
  - Clear the `test` attribute field and submit the form.
  - The Account UI should now be displayed. The `test` attribute value displayed there is still "anything". The same applies when displaying the user in the Admin UI. 

### Anything else?

Note that I've tested this with version 23.0.7 and current main branch. Probably this also occurs with version 24.x.

**UPDATE**: Clearing values with the Account UI and Admin UI works fine. The issue seems to be restricted to the Login Theme.
commit_message:

Closes #27797

issue_title: Can't register webauthn passwordless key when RS1 signature algorithm is configured in policies
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication/webauthn

### Describe the bug

While using the "RS1" signature algorithm in webauthn passwordless policies, I can't register a new webauthn Key. 

I received an error message in the browser console when loading the page : "Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected number". Additionally, when attempting to register the key, I received the following error message: "Uncaught ReferenceError: registerSecurityKey is not defined at HTMLInputElement.onclick (required-action?execution=webauthn-register-passwordless&client_id=...)".

Upon reviewing the HTML file loaded by "/login-actions/required-action?execution=webauthn-register-passwordless&client_id=...", I noticed an error on line "let signatureAlgorithms =[-36,-257,-7,-35,-258,-259,-65 535,]".

### Version

23.0.6

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I can register a webauthn passwordless key when signature algorithm "RS1" is configured in webauthn passwordless policies

### Actual behavior

I can't register a webauth passwordless key when signature algorithm "RS1" is configured in webauthn passwordless policies

### How to Reproduce?

1. Add "RS1" in "signature algorithm" field in "webauthn passwordless policies" authentication configuration.
2. Add "Webauthn Register Passwordless" required action on a user
3. Connect with this user and try to add a new webauthn key

### Anything else?

I suspect the issue was introduced by the "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/1af4a7a5325c248da2275759379ec00e495045df" commit.

issue_title: Incorrect Length Validation for Attribute
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/cli

### Describe the bug

The 'phone' attribute is configured to require arbitrary validation conditions, specifically a length between 13 and 20 characters  (regardless of trim-disabled). However, executing the provided command with a 13-character phone attribute :
kcadm.sh create users -r myrealm -s username=Someone -s email=someone@example.com -s enabled=true -s emailVerified=true -s attributes.firstName=Foo -s attributes.lastName=Bar -s attributes.phone='0123456789012'
Results in the error mentioned down. The condition should be satisfied with 13 characters, and the command should succeed.

If an extra character is added to the 'phone' attribute, making it 14 characters, the command passes successfully. This behavior contradicts the specified length requirement and suggests a counting error in the tool. Notably, through the admin UI, a 13-character text passes validation successfully.

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

successful user creation: Created new user with id 'xxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxx'
with output to stdout, not to stderror and maybe formated as JSON or GUID only. 

### Actual behavior

Results with the following error: 
error-invalid-length

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure a realm with a mandatory 'phone' attribute requiring arbitrary validation conditions, specifically a length between 13 and 20 characters.
2. Execute the provided command with a 13-character 'phone' attribute.
3. (Optional Step) Execute the provided command with a 14-character 'phone' attribute. 

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Actual behavior

While trying to reproduce this issue locally running the testsuite using private account, the application fails with the very same error.

The login starts by clicking on the Stack Overflow social login option, then it's redirected to the Stack Overflow login page, and after inserting the login credentials, the application is not able to redirect back to Keycloak and fails with:

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/67582554/5e91630c-8e36-479d-88f9-6701100f0336)

Similar behaviour occurred in #27197 but in the end the failure was not a regression and purely related to the PayPal IdP.

<ins>However</ins>, while trying to manually login via Stack Overflow having Keycloak running, the application successfully proceeds with the login to Keycloak. The failure occurs <ins>only</ins> during the execution of the testsuite.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Set up the Stack Overflow application according to the documentation 
    https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_stackoverflow
3. Prepare `social.properties` file containing the required variables (e.g., Client ID, Client Secret, ...)
4. Run the testsuite with following command with your custom `social.properties` file
```
$ mvn -f testsuite/integration-arquillian/pom.xml clean install \
      -Dtest=SocialLoginTest#stackoverflowLogin \
      -Dsocial.config=path\to\social.properties \
      -Dbrowser=chrome
```

### Anything else?

Related resources:
- [Server Administration Guide | Social Indentity Providers | Stack Overflow](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_stackoverflow)
- [Authentication - Stack Exchange API](https://api.stackexchange.com/docs/authentication)

- certificates used by stackexchange (stackoverflow social login)

Closes #27856

issue_title: SAMLIdentityProvider not honoring SamlAuthenticationPreprocessor
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

In [`SAMLIdentityProvider.performLogin`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java) 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L125

when an `AuthnRequestType` is created 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L194

and possibly customized with available `SamlAuthenticationPreprocessor.beforeSendingLoginRequest`

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L195

such `authnRequest` instance is discarded and a new one is created with `authnRequestBuilder.toDocument()`

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L207

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L207

It can easily fixed by using the created `SAML2Request.convert(authnRequest)` in place a brand-new one (PR will follow) as it's correctly already done in `keycloakInitiatedBrowserLogout`

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L312

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L314


### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

In `SAMLIdentityProvider.performLogin` existing `authnRequest` should be used by simply passing it over converted with `SAML2Request.convert(authnRequest)` as it's (correctly) already done in `keycloakInitiatedBrowserLogout`

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L312

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9ad447390a9b46d208ad7ea391afe83fd9c8e95b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L314

### Actual behavior

In `SAMLIdentityProvider.performLogin` existing `authnRequest` is not used.

### How to Reproduce?

Add a new new `SamlAuthenticationPreprocessor` and see how changes are not applied since discarded due to the above.

### Anything else?

Useful for context: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/9441

fixes SAMLIdentityProvider not honoring SamlAuthenticationPreprocessor (keycloak/keycloak#27875)

issue_title: Error when executing refresh grant, with scope param, without offline_access scope specified
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When executing an OIDC refresh grant request to the token endpoint, providing a valid offline token, and specifying scope via the scope parameter but without the offline_access scope included, the refresh request fails with error.

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Refresh request should succeed.

### Actual behavior

The following error is produced:
```json
{
    "error": "not_allowed",
    "error_description": "Offline tokens not allowed for the user or client"
}
```

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a client with offline_access scope available:
* standard flow enabled
* full scope allowed should be toggled off
* offline_access scope set as optional

2. Execute standard auth flow, with offline_access scope provided.
3. Copy refresh (offline) token provided by token endpoint.
4. Execute refresh token request providing offline token and scope in the form data. The scope should not specify offline_access.
```bash
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
--data-urlencode 'grant_type=refresh_token' \
--data-urlencode 'refresh_token={refresh_token}' \
--data-urlencode 'client_id=std-auth-client' \
--data-urlencode 'scope=openid'
```

Observe error is produced.

### Anything else?

Error thrown here:
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/1788cf2b092130adcc478e34f2d60f5447152369/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/TokenManager.java#L1154

in isOfflineTokenAllowed, seems the offline_access role may not be available on the client session context here:
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/1788cf2b092130adcc478e34f2d60f5447152369/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/UserSessionManager.java#L131
commit_message:

issue_title: Entry 999.0.0 in MIGRATION_MODEL prevents future migrations of the database
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When the table `MIGRATION_MODEL` contains an entry `999.0.0`, this prevents any future schema changes to the database.

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/23980#issuecomment-1999671099

As the Keycloak Operator 24.0.0 was released with an error that installed the nightly release of Keycloak, this might now happen to other people as well.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak startup should fail if an entry `999.0.0` exists in the migration model, and the current version is a release version, as something is very wrong when this happens.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak starts, but will never run a migration.

### How to Reproduce?

* Start an (old) Keycloak nightly
* Start a released version of Keycloak with a later date -> no migrations will run

### Anything else?

The remedy to such an entry might be tricky: Deleting the `999.0.0` entry might work, but it might actually be better to change it to the latest released version of Keycloak for that nightly build, as some migrations will have been run. 

It would also be an idea to create one entry for each migration run, not only the latest migration run, so it would make a repair simpler, where one would need to delete only the `999.0.0` entry.
commit_message:
Entry 999.0.0 in MIGRATION_MODEL prevents future migrations of the database

Closes #27941

issue_title: Passkey "Avoid same authenticator registration" doesn't work
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication/webauthn

### Describe the bug

The "Avoid same authenticator registration" setting (under Authentication > Webauthn Passwordless Policy) doesn't seem to work when registering new passkey credentials.

### Version

24.0.1 (also observed in 23.0.6)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Enabling the setting should prevent the WebAuthn Authenticator from creating a new credential and also avoid saving a duplicate credential in the WebAuthn Relying Party (i.e. Keycloak).

### Actual behavior

Enabling the setting does not prevent the WebAuthn Authenticator from creating a new credential and also saves a duplicate credential in the WebAuthn Relying Party (i.e. Keycloak).

### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable the "Avoid same authenticator registration" setting under Authentication > Webauthn Passwordless Policy.
2. Set up a passkey for a new/existing user (either from account console or via a required action). **Prerequisite:** You must have the "WebAuthn Register Passwordless" required action enabled for the realm in Authentication tab.
3. Set up another passkey for the user (either from account console or via a required action).
4. Notice a duplicate passkey is created (in both the authenticator and in Keycloak).

### Anything else?

The expectation is based on the description of [excludeCredentials](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CredentialsContainer/create#excludecredentials) (see also https://web.dev/articles/webauthn-exclude-credentials) which this setting seems to configure.

**Browser:** macOS Safari Version 17.4

**Authenticators:**
- Mac running macOS 14.4 (23E214)
- iPhone running iOS 17.3.1 acting as a cross-device authenticator for the above Mac.

Did not check other authenticators.

All other settings in Webauthn Passwordless Policy are set to their default values.

Closes #27945

issue_title: ORA-01450 when updating keycloak 23 -> 24
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When updating from 23.0.7 to 24.0.1, liquibase fails to create an index and stops execution of keycloak.
I assume, this happens, because in my oracle installation the data type of dbms_lob.substr(VALUE,255,1) is NVARCHAR2(16383). This is more, that my oracle installation can handle as key length for an index.
Can we cast the length down to something compatible?

Here is my error message:
{"timestamp":"2024-03-14T16:27:56.960608219Z","sequence":11390,
 "loggerClassName":"org.jboss.logging.Logger",
 "loggerName":"org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler",
 "level":"ERROR",
 "message":"ERROR: Migration failed for changeset META-INF/jpa-changelog-24.0.0.xml::24.0.0-26618-reindex::keycloak:\n     Reason: liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: ORA-01450: maximum key length (6397) exceeded\n\nhttps://docs.oracle.com/error-help/db/ora-01450/ [**Failed SQL: (1450) CREATE INDEX KEYCLOAK_MASTER_TEST.IDX_CLIENT_ATT_BY_NAME_VALUE ON KEYCLOAK_MASTER_TEST.CLIENT_ATTRIBUTES(NAME, _dbms_lob.substr(VALUE,255,1)_)**]",
 "threadName":"main","threadId":1,"mdc":{},"ndc":"","hostName":"keycloak-7788768f78-96f5p",
 "processName":"QuarkusEntryPoint","processId":379}

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Liquibase updates the database and the server starts.

### Actual behavior

Liquibase aborts and stops the server.

### How to Reproduce?

Run keycloak on an Oracle database.
Have NLS_NCHAR_CHARACTERSET set to AL16UTF16.

### Anything else?

Oracle's explanation to this setting is:
The maximum value is:
**16383 if MAX_STRING_SIZE = EXTENDED and the national character set is AL16UTF16**
32767 if MAX_STRING_SIZE = EXTENDED and the national character set is UTF8
2000 if MAX_STRING_SIZE = STANDARD and the national character set is AL16UTF16
4000 if MAX_STRING_SIZE = STANDARD and the national character set is UTF8
commit_message:
ORA-01450 error for index IDX_CLIENT_ATT_BY_NAME_VALUE in oracle when MAX_STRING_SIZE is EXTENDED
Closes #27967

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: User Profile: Inconsistent ordering of attributes between account and login themes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area login/ui ### Describe the bug The order of attributes is inconsistend between the account console and login theme (registration page, verify user profile, etc.). 1. Specifically, the account console puts all attributes without a group first and groups them in a section labelled "General". The login theme puts all attributes without a group last, visually appearing as part of the last named group. 2. ~~Additionally, attribute groups are sorted by (the translation key of the) display name in the login theme, and are unsorted in the account console (using the order they are returned by the API, which seems to match creation order of the groups).~~ _Split into separate issue #27994_ ### Version 24.0.1 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Ordering of attributes should be the same in the account console and login theme forms. ### Actual behavior
1. Attributes without a group

User profile configuration: ![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/5558742/7c0bc0db-f5a3-4747-822f-5eae95e28072) Account console: ![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/5558742/c26994ff-af7d-41a5-9caf-62382448a1f6) Registration page: ![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/5558742/a36b1cad-884c-450c-be52-7b6b07d83601)

2. Manually created "General" group

Attribute groups configuration: ![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/5558742/5cb60dd3-a033-4609-9a36-0972a728ba32) Account console: ![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/5558742/b9b5b5b2-85eb-4d4f-9eb2-e21c41d0fb54) Registration page: ![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/5558742/e4a0ed56-722e-411a-a301-247413aa0282)

### How to Reproduce? 1. Create a new realm and a test account 2. Go to the user profile configuration 3. Add a custom attribute group, create at least one custom attribute and assign it to the new group. Leave the default attributes without a group. 4. Observe the order of the attributes on the registration page and the account console. 5. Create a second attribute group, use `${aaaGeneral}` or similar as the display name (so that it will be sorted first alphabetically). Assign all 4 default attributes to that group. 6. Observe the order of the attributes on the registration page and the account console again. ### Anything else? **Regarding 1:** In my opinion, the ordering in the account console makes more sense. General attributes without a group (including the 4 default attributes) are the most important ones and should appear first. Additionally, I think it would make sense that attributes without a group appear first without a group header in the login theme. In the common case that no custom attributes are defined, it wouldn't make sense to add a "General" heading to the default attributes. And even with custom attributes in their own group, a heading for the common fields like name and email doesn't really add information. You can always add a heading by creating your own attribute group and assigning the default attributes to it. Manually creating an attribute group for the default attributes seems like a workaround, but has its own issues: * You run into different inconsitent ordering between groups (see 2). * You're unable to display attributes without a heading in the login theme, which is the default behavior and commonly a wanted behavior. * In the account console, the locale attribute will remain in the default "General" section separated from the custom "General" section. **Regarding 2:** Sorting attribute groups by the translation key is a bit unexpected, but at least consistent. You can enforce the ordering you want by strategically naming your translation keys. The best solution would be to add an explicit ordering to attribute groups, or use the already existing ordering of the attributes themselves to sort the groups, but that would be an additional feature request. **Relevant code:** Ordering in login theme: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/28afd77917612ffed8090902315eaa6485455645/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/forms/login/freemarker/model/AbstractUserProfileBean.java#L33-L46 Ordering in account console: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d61b1ddb09c5d1396633504c7dbb0ccdc91b3f8a/js/libs/ui-shared/src/user-profile/UserProfileFields.tsx#L114-L118
commit_message:
Attributes without a group should appear first

In the login theme, user profile attributes that
are not assigned to an attribute group should
appear before all other attributes. This aligns
the login theme (registration, verify profile,
etc.) with the account and admin console.

Fixes #27981

Signed-off-by: René Zeidler <rene.zeidler@gmx.de>

issue_title: JWK key ignored due to missing required field 'use' despite matching KID
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When configuring a client with **private_key_jwt** authentication method, we can specify a **JWKS URL** do make keycloak automatically download the keys when an unknown KID is seen during client authentication.

But if the key provided by the jwks endpoint has no field "use", it is ignored even if the kid matched and the authentication fails as shown in the following traces : 
```
[org.keycloak.util.JWKSUtils] Ignoring JWK key '<MyKID>'. Missing required field 'use'
And later :
[org.keycloak.keys.infinispan.InfinispanPublicKeyStorageProvider] public key wasn't found in the storage. Requested kid " <MyKID>.
```
The jwks endpoint I used had only one key available.

### Version

24

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The key provided by the endpoint that matches the jwt header KID of the assertion submitted by the client is used no regard of the use parameter field.

According to : [section 4.2 of RFC-7517](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7517#section-4.2), the use parameter is optional.
```
 "use" (Public Key Use) Parameter

   The "use" (public key use) parameter identifies the intended use of
   the public key.  The "use" parameter is employed to indicate whether
   a public key is used for encrypting data or verifying the signature
   on data.

   Values defined by this specification are:

   o  "sig" (signature)
   o  "enc" (encryption)

   Other values MAY be used.  The "use" value is a case-sensitive
   string.  Use of the "use" member is OPTIONAL, unless the application
   requires its presence. 
```

Given the fact that the assertion jwt header provided by the client contains a _kid_ and an _algorithm_, it is sufficient to identify the key as it should be unique within a keyset.

### Actual behavior

The key is ignored.

### How to Reproduce?

- Create a jwks endpoint that publishes only one public key 
- Ensure that key doesn't have the use attribute.
- Configure a client to use that endpoint
- Authenticate using private_key_jwt method.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Allow empty key use in JWKS for client authentication

Closes #28004

Signed-off-by: Justin Tay <49700559+justin-tay@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: Firefox Webauthn Registration "SecurityError: The operation is insecure."
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication/webauthn

### Describe the bug

on firefox on windows 11 if i try to configure webauthn by following the [documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/21.1.2/server_admin/#webauthn_server_administration_guide) i fail with this error when i go to webauthn registering phase. but on all other website like google, webauthn.io it's work fine...

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/8479023/a08e286b-0d67-4f1f-b9f0-f7cc884e15c2)

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/8479023/497c4667-cd67-44e3-8544-d86e93a7d266)

after digging on the web find why.... i've not found why...

finaly i've tested on different browser ... and with debugger console...
and on edge finaly i seen this message in the dev console (i've no message on firefox) :
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/8479023/396e01a7-6349-4233-9c07-7f41c0fa5133)

the message speek about this link : https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/main/content/browser/webauth/pub_key_cred_params.md

on this page they say that on microsoft windows platform need to have Signature algorithms RS256 in [pubKeyCredParams](https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn-2/#dictdef-publickeycredentialparameters)... and keycloak by default not use this Signature algorithms (only ES256)...

after adding it in keycloak admin console: 
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/8479023/1ad4e81c-1991-479a-b62e-9ae50a64654d)

we don't have any more the error message in edge, and it's work, and it's work also on firefox...

### Version

21.1.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Webauthn Registration without error...

### Actual behavior

Webauthn Registration "SecurityError: The operation is insecure."

### How to Reproduce?

install keycloak 21.1.2 from scratch and follow the documentation https://www.keycloak.org/docs/21.1.2/server_admin/#webauthn_server_administration_guide and try to log first time and try to register webauthn key...

### Anything else?

i think you must add RS256 by default in addition of ES256, because of windows platform authenticator that require it... or modify the documentation to inform keycloak admin user to add this Signature algorithms if they have windows user...
commit_message:
add RS256 algorithm to webauthn default policy (#30528)

closes #28020 

Signed-off-by: Mathieu CARBONNEAUX <mathieu.carbonneaux@ch2o.info>

issue_title: inputOptionLabels not used for OptionComponent in ui-shared

### Describe the bug

Create attribute with inputType multiselect-checkboxes or select-radiobuttons

 {

        "options": {
          "options": [
